questions.question: Explain the three main types of radio wave propagation. Give one example of each.
keyPoints: Ground wave propagation: follows Earth's curvature, used for long-wave radio
Sky wave propagation: uses ionosphere reflection, used for AM radio and shortwave
Line-of-sight propagation: direct path between antennas, used for microwaves and TV

questions.question: What are the advantages and disadvantages of satellite communication?
keyPoints: Advantages: wide coverage area, can reach remote locations, broadcast capability
Disadvantages: high latency (delay), expensive to launch, signal can be affected by weather, security concerns

questions.question: Compare radio waves and microwaves in terms of propagation characteristics and applications.
keyPoints: Radio waves: can penetrate walls, longer distances, lower frequencies, used in broadcasting
Microwaves: line-of-sight required, shorter distances, higher frequencies, used in point-to-point communication

questions.question: Describe the Bluetooth technology and its applications in daily life.
keyPoints: Bluetooth operates at 2.4 GHz, uses FHSS, short-range (10m), low power
Applications: wireless headphones, speaker connections, file transfer, smart home devices, car hands-free

questions.question: What is line-of-sight propagation? Explain why it is necessary for microwave communication.
keyPoints: Line-of-sight: direct path between transmitter and receiver without obstacles
Microwaves travel in straight lines and cannot bend around obstacles
Requires tall antennas or elevated positions to overcome Earth's curvature
Obstructions like buildings or mountains block the signal

questions.question: Explain the difference between Wi-Fi (802.11) and Bluetooth (802.15) standards.
keyPoints: Wi-Fi: longer range (~100m), higher power consumption, higher data rates, infrastructure-based
Bluetooth: shorter range (~10m), lower power, lower data rates, ad-hoc/peer-to-peer
Different frequency bands and modulation techniques

questions.question: What are the limitations of infrared communication in computer networks?
keyPoints: Requires line-of-sight between transmitter and receiver
Cannot penetrate walls or solid objects
Limited to short distances
Susceptible to interference from sunlight and bright lights
Lower bandwidth compared to other wireless technologies

questions.question: Explain the concept of electromagnetic spectrum and its division for wireless communication. Discuss the frequency ranges used for different wireless technologies.
keyPoints: Electromagnetic spectrum: entire range of electromagnetic wave frequencies
Division into radio waves, microwaves, infrared, visible light, UV, X-rays, gamma rays
Radio waves (3 kHz - 300 GHz) subdivided into VLF, LF, MF, HF, VHF, UHF, SHF, EHF
Microwaves (300 MHz - 300 GHz) used for satellite, radar, WLAN
Infrared (300 GHz - 400 THz) used for remote controls, short-range communication
Each range has different propagation characteristics and applications

questions.question: Describe the working of satellite communication systems. Explain the different types of satellite orbits and their applications.
keyPoints: Satellite communication uses microwave signals to/from artificial satellites
Components: ground station, uplink, transponder, downlink
Orbit types: GEO (35,786 km) - fixed position, for TV broadcasting
MEO (2,000-35,786 km) - GPS, navigation
LEO (200-2,000 km) - Iridium, global phone, low latency
Advantages: wide coverage, global reach, broadcast capability
Disadvantages: latency, cost, weather sensitivity

questions.question: Discuss the various wireless LAN standards (IEEE 802.11) and their characteristics. Include frequency bands, data rates, and typical applications.
keyPoints: 802.11a: 5 GHz, up to 54 Mbps, OFDM
802.11b: 2.4 GHz, up to 11 Mbps, DSSS
802.11g: 2.4 GHz, up to 54 Mbps, backward compatible with b
802.11n: 2.4/5 GHz, up to 600 Mbps, MIMO
802.11ac: 5 GHz, up to 3.46 Gbps, MU-MIMO
802.11ax (Wi-Fi 6): 2.4/5 GHz, up to 9.6 Gbps, OFDMA
Each standard evolved for higher data rates and better efficiency

questions.question: Explain the principles of spread spectrum communication. Compare FHSS and DSSS techniques with their advantages.
keyPoints: Spread spectrum: spreads signal over wide frequency band
Originally developed for military communication (jamming resistance)
FHSS: hops between frequencies using pseudorandom sequence
DSSS: spreads signal using pseudonoise code directly on carrier
Advantages: interference resistance, security, multiple users
Bluetooth uses FHSS, Wi-Fi 802.11b uses DSSS
Trade-offs: bandwidth efficiency vs interference resistance
